title: Coded Unfair: AI Bias Exposed

modules.title: The Algorithm That Chose Wrong
modules.sub: Meet the AI system failing the same people repeatedly
modules.description: Learners are dropped into a city scenario where an AI decision-making system is producing outcomes that consistently disadvantage one group. They examine the system's stated purpose against its actual results, and begin building a case file to track the investigation.

modules.title: Where Bias Hides in Data
modules.sub: Trace unfairness back to its source in training data
modules.description: Learners dig into the dataset that trained the system, looking for patterns of underrepresentation, historical discrimination, and flawed labelling. They identify specific moments in the data pipeline where bias was introduced or amplified.

modules.title: Test the System: Find the Gap
modules.sub: Run structured tests to surface the performance gap
modules.description: Learners design and apply a bias audit framework, comparing the system's outputs across different demographic groups. They document the disparity they find and evaluate whether it meets a threshold that should trigger intervention.

modules.title: Real People, Real Harm
modules.sub: Connect algorithmic failures to lived human consequences
modules.description: Learners encounter case profiles of people directly affected by the system's biased decisions — covering areas like housing, healthcare, or employment. They analyse the type and severity of harm caused, adding a human impact assessment to their audit report.

modules.title: Fix It or Scrap It?
modules.sub: Weigh the options and propose a reasoned intervention
modules.description: Learners evaluate a range of responses — from retraining the model and adding oversight mechanisms to suspending or replacing the system entirely. They weigh tradeoffs around accuracy, fairness, cost, and urgency, then commit to and justify a specific course of action.

modules.title: Present Your Bias Audit
modules.sub: Deliver your findings and recommendation to decision-makers
modules.description: Learners compile their investigation into a structured bias audit report and present it as if addressing a civic panel or company board. They defend their methodology, evidence, and proposed intervention, responding to challenge questions that test the strength of their reasoning.

synopsis: An AI system in your city is making decisions that keep failing the same group of people â and you've been asked to investigate. You'll follow the bias from the training data all the way to real-world impact, then decide what should actually be done about it.
rationale: Older teens are approaching civic life and need analytical tools to recognize how algorithmic bias encodes and amplifies systemic inequity in hiring, policing, healthcare, and education.
learning_outcome: Students can conduct a structured bias audit of a described AI system, identify the source of unfairness in the pipeline, and propose a documented, reasoned intervention or alternative.
